title: The Murderbot Diaries
author: Martha Wells
reason: A sci-fi series about a humanoid security bot that hacks its own governor module (the module that forces it to always obey human commands) and becomes free. We follow its adventures and often hilarious interactions with humans as a self-aware, autonomous being. There's also a season on Amazon Prime worth watching, and it's really funny.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
reason: Pitched as a must-read on the non-fiction side. It ties into our discussion of social experiments like the Bystander Effect and the Diffusion of Responsibility, and how those same dynamics quietly affect teams within a company.

title: Antifragile
author: Nassim Nicholas Taleb
reason: Recommended alongside Thinking, Fast and Slow as an essential non-fiction read, about things that gain from disorder, volatility and stress rather than breaking under them.

title: Taiko
author: Eiji Yoshikawa
reason: Presented by a member currently exploring Japanese culture. Set in the age of the samurai, it follows how three figures in 16th-century Japan ended the civil war and brought about two centuries of peace.

title: The Last Messiah
author: Peter Wessel Zapffe
reason: An essay by the Norwegian philosopher exploring antinatalism and the burden of human consciousness. We began discussing it but ran out of time before we could finish.
